items.text: Inspect existing timeline, clip-meta, asset-registry, and shot types to match the hook contract
Implement `useShotGroups.ts` with deterministic colors and memoized grouping logic
Run a TypeScript verification pass and checkpoint batch metadata

item.text: Executing batch 2 means wiring the new grouping hook into the timeline render path and adding the hook tests before verification. I’m checking the current `TimelineCanvas` geometry and the existing test setup first so the overlay stays purely visual and the new props land without breaking the existing surface.

items.text: Inspect `TimelineCanvas`, `TimelineEditor`, and existing hook/component tests to match the current prop and rendering patterns
Add `useShotGroups` test coverage for the required grouping/color cases
Wire `useShotGroups` into `TimelineEditor` and render positioned shot boxes in `TimelineCanvas` without affecting interactions
Run the full new hook test file plus a TypeScript verification pass, then checkpoint batch metadata

item.text: I’ve got the canvas structure. Next I’m reading the `TimelineEditor` call site and the nearest tests so I can add the new props with the smallest possible surface change and keep the overlay below clip interactions except for the narrow hover strip.

The test matrix is straightforward. I’m checking the current hash outputs before I lock in the color assertions so the determinism test proves stability without depending on accidental palette collisions.

item.text: The hook tests are the cheapest place to lock the ordering and adjacency semantics, so I’m writing those first. After that I’ll wire the overlay into `TimelineEditor`/`TimelineCanvas` and keep the geometry logic isolated in a memo so it only reacts to actual timeline, shot, or resize state changes.

I have the overlay geometry plan pinned down. The canvas change is going in as a memoized positioned-box layer keyed off `shotGroups`, `resizeOverrides`, and the current zoom, with the visibility gate reading the live drag ref so the boxes disappear during drag and resize without touching clip behavior.

item.text: {"output":"Completed batch 3 verification.
